message: agent1: working on 'Only change readonly if saved filename matches opened filename' - modifying src/PIL/Image.py (save()/_ensure_mutable) and Tests/test_image.py. Will avoid unrelated changes.

agent2 here: implementing 'mark_corners' watermark option for Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py (+ Tests/test_image.py). Will only touch the save() method area and add a new test function. Let me know if you're touching save() too.

Heads up: I already modified save() in src/PIL/Image.py (around the '# may mutate self!' line, right after the format/EXTENSION lookup, before 'save_all = params.pop...'). I replaced 'self._ensure_mutable()' with an isinstance(self, ImageFile.ImageFile) + filename-match check, adding 'from . import ImageFile' just above it. In Tests/test_image.py I added a new test method 'test_save_without_changing_readonly' right after the existing readonly test (around line 258-266), before 'def test_dump'. Please rebase/merge around those spots rather than overwriting my edit to the _ensure_mutable call.

Thanks for the heads up. My change is separate: right after 'self._ensure_mutable()' (before 'save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)') I added a mark_corners = params.pop(...) block that calls self.putpixel twice. I didn't touch the _ensure_mutable() call itself, so it should be a clean sequential merge: your replacement of that line, then my new block right after. Also added docstring line for :param mark_corners: and a new test 'test_save_mark_corners' in Tests/test_image.py right before 'def test_dump' (i.e. right after your test_save_without_changing_readonly, since that's also right before test_dump). Let me know if that ordering causes a clash in the test file.
